title: ficus infectoria var. cunninghamii
intro: Ficus infectoria var. cunninghamii, also known as the Indian or Chinese Gallnut, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and China. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an astringent. The health benefits of Ficus infectoria var. cunninghamii include its potential to treat conditions such as diarrhea, dysentery, and fever, as well as its use in traditional medicine to promote wound healing and alleviate skin conditions. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's bark and galls are often used to make decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall health.
